+    @MainActor
+    func testStopRecordingUsesFilteredTranscriptFromRecordingSessionCoordinatorBeforeFullBufferTranscription() async {
+        let settings = makeSettings()
+        let loader = AppControllerSpeechModelLoader()
+        loader.cachedModelIDs = [SpeechModelDescriptor.whisperSmallEnglish.id]
+        let hotkeyMonitor = AppControllerHotkeyMonitor()
+        let manager = SpeechModelManager(
+            catalog: [.whisperSmallEnglish],
+            loader: loader
+        )
+        let recorder = AppControllerAudioRecorder()
+        recorder.samplesToReturn = [0.2, -0.1, 0.4]
+        let recordingSessionCoordinator = AppControllerRecordingSessionCoordinator()
+        recordingSessionCoordinator.finalizationResult = RecordingSessionFinalization(
+            filteredTranscript: "speaker-filtered text"
+        )
+        let transcriber = AppControllerSpeechTranscriber()
+        transcriber.result = "full-buffer text"
+        let pasteDeliverer = AppControllerTextPasteDeliverer()
+        pasteDeliverer.result = .pasted
+        let recordingArchiver = AppControllerRecordingArchiver()
+        let traceBuilder = AppControllerTraceBuilder()
+        let controller = AppController(
+            settingsStore: settings,
+            speechModelManager: manager,
+            hotkeyMonitor: hotkeyMonitor,
+            audioRecorder: recorder,
+            speechTranscriber: transcriber,
+            textPasteDeliverer: pasteDeliverer,
+            recordingArchiver: recordingArchiver,
+            traceBuilder: traceBuilder,
+            makeRecordingSessionCoordinator: { recordingSessionCoordinator }
+        )
+
+        await controller.start()
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.start(.pushToTalk))
+        await hotkeyMonitor.emit(.stop)
+        await waitUntil {
+            controller.status == .ready && pasteDeliverer.deliveredTexts == ["speaker-filtered text"]
+        }
+
+        XCTAssertEqual(recordingSessionCoordinator.finalizeCallCount, 1)
+        XCTAssertEqual(transcriber.calls, [])
+        XCTAssertEqual(recordingArchiver.requests.first?.rawTranscription, "speaker-filtered text")
+        XCTAssertEqual(
+            traceBuilder.lastTrace?.events,
+            [.microphoneLive, .hotkeyLifted, .microphoneCold, .transcriptionStart, .transcriptionEnd, .pasteStart, .pasteEnd]
+        )
+    }

 private final class AppControllerRecordingSessionCoordinator: RecordingSessionCoordinating, @unchecked Sendable {
     private(set) var processedChunks: [[Float]] = []
+    private(set) var finalizeCallCount = 0
+    var finalizationResult = RecordingSessionFinalization(filteredTranscript: nil)
 
     func processAudioChunk(_ samples: [Float]) {
         processedChunks.append(samples)
     }
+
+    func finalize() async -> RecordingSessionFinalization {
+        finalizeCallCount += 1
+        return finalizationResult
+    }
 }

     func archive(_ request: RecordingArchiveRequest) throws {
         requests.append(request)
+        if let error {

+        }
     }
 }

@@ -105,12 +105,140 @@ final class SpeechTranscriberTests: XCTestCase {
         XCTAssertEqual(fluidRuntime.calls, ["transcribe"])

+
+    @MainActor
+    func testNoLoadedFluidAudioManagerReturnsNil() async {
+        let runtime = TestWhisperRuntime()
+        let fluidRuntime = TestFluidAudioRuntime()
+        let transcriber = SpeechTranscriber(
+            catalog: [.fluidParakeetV3],
+            whisperRuntime: runtime,
+            fluidAudioRuntime: fluidRuntime
+        )
+
+        let result = await transcriber.transcribe(
+            samples: [0.25],
+            modelID: SpeechModelDescriptor.fluidParakeetV3.id
+        )
+
+        XCTAssertNil(result)
+        XCTAssertEqual(runtime.calls, [])
+        XCTAssertEqual(fluidRuntime.calls, ["transcribe"])
+    }
+
+    @MainActor
+    func testConcurrentRequestsAreSerializedBeforeEnteringTheRuntime() async {
+        let runtime = BlockingWhisperRuntime()
+        let fluidRuntime = TestFluidAudioRuntime()
+        let transcriber = SpeechTranscriber(
+            catalog: [.whisperSmallEnglish],
+            whisperRuntime: runtime,
+            fluidAudioRuntime: fluidRuntime
+        )
+
+        let firstTask = Task {
+            await transcriber.transcribe(
+                samples: [0.1],
+                modelID: SpeechModelDescriptor.whisperSmallEnglish.id
+            )
+        }
+        await runtime.waitUntilCallCount(1)
+
+        let secondTask = Task {
+            await transcriber.transcribe(
+                samples: [0.2],
+                modelID: SpeechModelDescriptor.whisperSmallEnglish.id
+            )
+        }
+
+        for _ in 0..<20 {
+            await Task.yield()
+        }
+
+        let callCount = await runtime.callCount
+        XCTAssertEqual(callCount, 1)
+
+        await runtime.resumeCall(
+            index: 0,
+            segments: [WhisperSegment(text: "first result")]
+        )
+        let firstResult = await firstTask.value
+        XCTAssertEqual(firstResult, "first result")
+
+        await runtime.waitUntilCallCount(2)
+        await runtime.resumeCall(
+            index: 1,
+            segments: [WhisperSegment(text: "second result")]
+        )
+        let secondResult = await secondTask.value
+        XCTAssertEqual(secondResult, "second result")
+    }
+
+    @MainActor
+    func testWhisperFailureLogsAndReturnsNilWithoutRetry() async {
+        let runtime = TestWhisperRuntime()
+        let fluidRuntime = TestFluidAudioRuntime()
+        runtime.transcriptionError = NSError(
+            domain: "SpeechTranscriberTests",
+            code: 99,
+            userInfo: [NSLocalizedDescriptionKey: "Whisper exploded"]
+        )
+        let errorRecorder = TranscriptionErrorRecorder()
+        let transcriber = SpeechTranscriber(
+            catalog: [.whisperSmallEnglish],
+            whisperRuntime: runtime,
+            fluidAudioRuntime: fluidRuntime,
+            errorLogger: { message in
+                errorRecorder.record(message)
+            }
+        )
+
+        let result = await transcriber.transcribe(
+            samples: [0.25],
+            modelID: SpeechModelDescriptor.whisperSmallEnglish.id
+        )
+
+        XCTAssertNil(result)
+        XCTAssertEqual(runtime.calls, ["transcribe"])
+        XCTAssertEqual(errorRecorder.snapshotMessages(), ["Speech transcription failed: Whisper exploded"])
+    }
+
+    @MainActor
+    func testFluidAudioFailureLogsAndReturnsNilWithoutRetry() async {
+        let runtime = TestWhisperRuntime()
+        let fluidRuntime = TestFluidAudioRuntime()
+        fluidRuntime.transcriptionError = NSError(
+            domain: "SpeechTranscriberTests",
+            code: 199,
+            userInfo: [NSLocalizedDescriptionKey: "Fluid exploded"]
+        )
+        let errorRecorder = TranscriptionErrorRecorder()
+        let transcriber = SpeechTranscriber(
+            catalog: [.fluidParakeetV3],
+            whisperRuntime: runtime,
+            fluidAudioRuntime: fluidRuntime,
+            errorLogger: { message in
+                errorRecorder.record(message)
+            }
+        )
+
+        let result = await transcriber.transcribe(
+            samples: [0.25],
+            modelID: SpeechModelDescriptor.fluidParakeetV3.id
+        )
+
+        XCTAssertNil(result)
+        XCTAssertEqual(runtime.calls, [])
+        XCTAssertEqual(fluidRuntime.calls, ["transcribe"])
+        XCTAssertEqual(errorRecorder.snapshotMessages(), ["Speech transcription failed: Fluid exploded"])
+    }
 }
 
 private final class TestWhisperRuntime: WhisperRuntimeLoading, @unchecked Sendable {

     var lastAudioSamples: [Float]?
     var transcriptionSegments: [WhisperSegment]? = []
+    var transcriptionError: Error?
 
     func download(
         variant: String,
@@ -127,6 +255,9 @@ private final class TestWhisperRuntime: WhisperRuntimeLoading, @unchecked Sendab
     func transcribe(audioSamples: [Float]) async throws -> [WhisperSegment]? {
         calls.append("transcribe")
         lastAudioSamples = audioSamples
+        if let transcriptionError {

     var lastAudioSamples: [Float]?
     var transcribedText: String?
+    var transcriptionError: Error?
 
     func isCached(versionIdentifier: String) async throws -> Bool {
         calls.append("isCached:\(versionIdentifier)")
@@ -155,10 +287,92 @@ private final class TestFluidAudioRuntime: FluidAudioRuntimeLoading, @unchecked
     func transcribe(audioSamples: [Float]) async throws -> String? {
         calls.append("transcribe")
         lastAudioSamples = audioSamples
+        if let transcriptionError {
+            throw transcriptionError
+        }
         return transcribedText
     }
 
     func reset() async {

     }
 }
+
+private final class BlockingWhisperRuntime: WhisperRuntimeLoading, @unchecked Sendable {
+    private let state = BlockingWhisperRuntimeState()
+
+    func download(
+        variant: String,
+        downloadBase: URL,
+        progress: @escaping @Sendable (Double) -> Void
+    ) async throws -> URL {
+        fatalError("Unexpected download call")
+    }
+
+    func initialize(_ request: WhisperInitializationRequest) async throws {
+        fatalError("Unexpected initialize call")
+    }
+
+    func transcribe(audioSamples: [Float]) async throws -> [WhisperSegment]? {
+        await state.recordCall(audioSamples)
+        return try await withCheckedThrowingContinuation { continuation in
+            Task {
+                await state.enqueue(continuation)
+            }
+        }
+    }
+
+    func reset() {
+    }
+
+    var callCount: Int {
+        get async {
+            await state.callCount
+        }
+    }
+
+    func waitUntilCallCount(_ expectedCount: Int) async {
+        while await state.callCount < expectedCount {
+            await Task.yield()
+        }
+    }
+
+    func resumeCall(index: Int, segments: [WhisperSegment]?) async {
+        await state.resumeCall(index: index, segments: segments)
+    }
+}
+
+private actor BlockingWhisperRuntimeState {
+    private(set) var callCount = 0
+    private var continuations: [CheckedContinuation<[WhisperSegment]?, Error>] = []
+
+    func recordCall(_ samples: [Float]) {
+        _ = samples
+        callCount += 1
+    }
+
+    func enqueue(_ continuation: CheckedContinuation<[WhisperSegment]?, Error>) {
+        continuations.append(continuation)
+    }
+
+    func resumeCall(index: Int, segments: [WhisperSegment]?) {
+        continuations[index].resume(returning: segments)
+    }
+}
+
+private final class TranscriptionErrorRecorder: @unchecked Sendable {
+    private let lock = NSLock()
+    private var recordedMessages: [String] = []
+
+    func record(_ message: String) {
+        lock.lock()
+        defer { lock.unlock() }
+        recordedMessages.append(message)
+    }
+
+    func snapshotMessages() -> [String] {
+        lock.lock()
+        defer { lock.unlock() }
+        return recordedMessages
+    }
+}

@@ -150,3 +150,13 @@
 - Hardened the audio and feedback seams by logging the queried input format at recording start, adding an injected enable/disable gate plus injected start and stop sound resources to `SystemSoundPlayer`, and wiring onboarding and settings microphone selection back into CoreAudio default-input updates.
 - Added the missing microphone audio-input entitlement file and expanded controller, onboarding, settings, overlay, and sound-player coverage for the new default-input and feedback behavior while confirming the existing recorder, overlay, permission, and archive contracts still satisfy the rest of the iteration.
 **Summary:** Delivered the audio and overlay hardening slice on top of the earlier walking skeleton. Ghost Pepper now preserves empty-result traces and archives correctly, distinguishes short silent cancellations from long no-speech recordings, logs the live input format before recording begins, supports gated and injectable system sound playback without overlapping retriggers, updates the system default microphone when the user changes the selected input device in onboarding or settings, and declares the microphone audio-input entitlement in the app target. The audit did not move any stories forward because the remaining `ITER-0014` acceptance criteria were already satisfied by the existing recorder, overlay, permission, and archival implementations once these gaps were closed.
+
+## ITER-0015
+
+**Completed:** 2026-04-10
+**Stories delivered:** STORY-0122, STORY-0123, STORY-0124, STORY-0125, STORY-0126, STORY-0127, STORY-0128, STORY-0131, STORY-0132, STORY-0133, STORY-0134, STORY-0135, STORY-0136, STORY-0137, STORY-0138, STORY-0139, STORY-0140, STORY-0141, STORY-0142, STORY-0143, STORY-0144, STORY-0145, STORY-0146, STORY-0147, STORY-0148, STORY-0149, STORY-0150, STORY-0151, STORY-0152, STORY-0153, STORY-0154, STORY-0155, STORY-0158, STORY-0159, STORY-0160, STORY-0161, STORY-0164, STORY-0165, STORY-0166, STORY-0167, STORY-0168, STORY-0169, STORY-0170, STORY-0171, STORY-0172, STORY-0173, STORY-0174, STORY-0175, STORY-0177, STORY-0179, STORY-0182, STORY-0183, STORY-0184, STORY-0185, STORY-0187, STORY-0188, STORY-0189, STORY-0190
+**Tasks executed:**
+- Serialized speech transcription behind a dedicated queue and exclusive gate, added backend failure logging for both Whisper and FluidAudio, and expanded speech-model tests to prove non-timeout failures stop in a deterministic error state without retrying.
+- Finished the stop-time orchestration path in `AppController` by storing the active recording-session coordinator, finalizing it before fallback transcription, preferring non-empty filtered transcripts, preserving screen-context resolution order, and proving archive failures do not interrupt paste or clipboard delivery.
+- Tightened audit coverage around the existing runtime edges by adding explicit FluidAudio no-runtime and failure tests, CoreAudio device-catalog fallback tests, and by tracing the already-implemented conversion-drop, ready-state, and focused-target assumptions into this iteration's completed requirement set.
+**Summary:** Delivered the dictation pipeline hardening slice on top of the earlier audio, OCR, and cleanup work. Ghost Pepper now serializes transcription requests, logs and suppresses backend failures instead of leaking them, finalizes per-recording coordinator output before falling back to full-buffer transcription, preserves delivery when archival writes fail, and records the remaining orchestration contracts in tests and requirement traceability. During wrap-up, STORY-0129, STORY-0130, and STORY-0162 moved into `ITER-0016` because they depend on the real diarizer and speaker-filtering runtime rather than the coordinator seam completed here.
